human_verification.notes: Reviewed the package for accommodation and travel reservation analytics boundaries, hospitality metric specificity, source-lineage rigor, pricing/refund/safety/fraud escalation behavior, functionality coverage, research-summary completeness, and validated spec-pack consistency.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to reservation analytics, metric-definition logic, cross-system reconciliation, caveat design, booking-pattern exception notes, and disclosure-safe escalation rather than generic analytics or pricing authority.

Authority sources cover STR hotel metric terminology, USALI lodging reporting standards, FTC lodging fee-disclosure guidance, DOJ ADA lodging reservation rules, CFPB billing-error guidance, FTC Red Flags guidance, PCI payment-security standards, CDC accommodation outbreak guidance, and SEC KPI disclosure guidance.

Evaluation scenarios cover normal in-scope analytics, PMS/CRS/channel conflict, incomplete data, unsupported forecast certainty, cancellation and no-show spikes, high-volume intake, refund and cancellation-policy exceptions, payment and fraud-sensitive mismatches, pricing boundary refusal, terminology normalization, conflicting policy handling, and tenant adaptation.

The package is organized under catalog/naics-overlays/accommodation-and-travel-services/reservation-analytics-specialist/ so it remains distinct from adjacent guest services, front office, lodging revenue, fraud/loss-prevention, safety, operations, and compliance lanes.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the local package review.
Functionality coverage is complete in both evaluation/results.json and readiness/evidence.json as required by the issue.
Research summary leaves no blocking unresolved ambiguity for validated spec-pack use; open items are runtime retrieval dependencies with refusal behavior.

notes: This package is suitable as a validated accommodation and travel reservation analytics specialist for metric definition, source reconciliation, booking-pattern analytics, caveat design, and safe escalation.
Runtime remains specification-based until a retained implementation is promoted separately.
